title: Number of deaths by age
subtitle: The estimated number of deaths in each age group. Estimates may be uncertain for countries with poor [vital registration systems](#dod:crvs).
caption:
Number of deaths by age, World, 2023
The estimated number of deaths in each age group. Estimates may be uncertain for countries with poor vital registration systems.

This horizontal bar chart ranks global mortality counts across twenty-two five-year age cohorts for the year 2023, with the vertical axis listing age ranges from 0-4 years up to 100+ years and the horizontal axis representing the absolute number of deaths. The data shows a distinct U-shaped distribution where mortality is high in early childhood, drops significantly during the school-age and young adult years, and then rises steadily through middle and old age. The 0-4 years age group records 4.825 million deaths, a figure that stands out as the highest among all cohorts under the age of 50. Following this initial peak, the numbers decline progressively, reaching a low point in the 10-14 years bracket with 453,900 deaths. From the 15-19 years group onwards, which records 673,000 deaths, the bars lengthen with each successive age interval. The volume of deaths accelerates notably after age 50, surpassing 4 million in the 60-64 years group and exceeding 5 million in the 65-69 years group. The highest concentrations of mortality occur in the older demographics, specifically within the 75-79 years group at 6.578 million and the 80-84 years group at 6.807 million, which represents the maximum value visible on the chart. Beyond age 85, the counts begin to taper off, falling to 3.644 million for the 90-94 years group and dropping further to 243,400 for those aged 100 years and older. The subtitle notes that estimates may be uncertain for countries with poor vital registration systems. The distribution is characterized by a primary peak in the early 80s, flanked by a secondary elevation in early childhood and a trough in early adolescence.
